strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|relative strength was clearly supportive|news/catalyst quality was mixed rather than decisive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-08 26.5|Sector trend does not fully support the thesis.|IV is very elevated for a long option.
decision_reasoning: CSCO is the cleanest long-only setup in the bundle: durable multi-week uptrend, positive RS vs SPY, and moderate IV (~43%) that keeps premium reasonable into a May 13 print. USAR and RKLB both carry triple-digit IV that punishes long premium, and RKLB's volume has collapsed to 32% of average. SPG has no usable option chain. The 98-strike May 22 call expires safely post-earnings and offers the best balance of breakeven distance and delta; the May 15 strike was avoided because it expires on the earnings date itself with unknown timing. Score lands in Standard recommend range — trend and IV are supportive, but sparse news coverage, slightly negative RS vs sector, and unknown earnings timing keep this short of a Strong call.
